content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `master...HEAD`, read the new review-classification implementation end-to-end, and ran `cargo test --quiet` successfully. I found 2 correctness gaps in the new schema/validation code.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:49-76` does not reject unknown or cross-variant fields during deserialization. Because `FindingClassification` is an internally tagged enum without any `deny_unknown_fields` guard, a payload like `{"classification":"fix-now",...,"mapped_to_bead_id":"bead-2"}` will deserialize as `FixNow` and silently discard the planned-elsewhere field. That violates the stated rule that each finding must have exactly one classification and lets malformed reviewer output take the wrong downstream path instead of being rejected.
   Rationale: This is a correctness issue in the core schema boundary: mixed or mistyped classifier payloads can be accepted as valid and then reconciled incorrectly.
2. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:127-143` never validates `remediation_hint` when it is present on `FixNow`. `Some("")` and whitespace-only hints currently pass `validate_classification`, even though the bead explicitly calls for rejecting malformed classifications and empty-string edge cases. That means persisted/classified findings can carry a meaningless remediation hint as if it were valid data.
   Rationale: This leaves one of the schema fields outside the domain-validation contract, so malformed `fix-now` findings are accepted instead of rejected.
